_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (prompt injection, insecure output handling, training data poisoning, model theft, excessive agency).
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Knowledge SP-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-001 (SP, cosine: 0.461), DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.428).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.480). Shared vocabulary: [applications, knowledge, vulnerabilities]. NICE-only terms [wireless] vs WIDAI-only terms [10, agency, data, excessive, handling, injection] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.878 (P=0.922, R=0.838). P exceeds R by 0.084 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Knowledge of wireless applications vulnerabilities" and "Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 f" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.558 > STS 0.480 (gap +0.078). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [applications, knowledge, vulnerabilities] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.4%. Shared tokens: [applications, knowledge, vulnerabilities]. NICE-only: [wireless]. WIDAI-only: [10, agency, data, excessive, handling, injection].
